verses.text: Blow the trumpet in Zion: 'the day of the LORD cometh,' a day of darkness; before the army is Eden, behind it a wilderness. Dread alarm. The judgment leaves devastation in its wake.
Blow the trumpet in Zion: 'the day of the LORD cometh,' a day of darkness; before the army is Eden, behind it a wilderness. Dread alarm. The judgment leaves devastation in its wake.
Blow the trumpet in Zion: 'the day of the LORD cometh,' a day of darkness; before the army is Eden, behind it a wilderness. Dread alarm. The judgment leaves devastation in its wake.
The invaders run like horses and chariots, leaping the walls, and 'all faces shall gather blackness.' Unstoppable. No defence avails against the army God sends.
The invaders run like horses and chariots, leaping the walls, and 'all faces shall gather blackness.' Unstoppable. No defence avails against the army God sends.
The invaders run like horses and chariots, leaping the walls, and 'all faces shall gather blackness.' Unstoppable. No defence avails against the army God sends.
They march in rank, climb the wall, and enter the houses 'like a thief.' Relentless. The judgment penetrates every refuge.
They march in rank, climb the wall, and enter the houses 'like a thief.' Relentless. The judgment penetrates every refuge.
They march in rank, climb the wall, and enter the houses 'like a thief.' Relentless. The judgment penetrates every refuge.
Earth quakes, heavens tremble, sun and moon darken; 'the day of the LORD is great and very terrible; who can abide it?' Awful day. None can stand before the Lord's reckoning in his own strength.
Earth quakes, heavens tremble, sun and moon darken; 'the day of the LORD is great and very terrible; who can abide it?' Awful day. None can stand before the Lord's reckoning in his own strength.
'Turn ye even to me with all your heart'; 'rend your heart, and not your garments,' for He is 'gracious and merciful.' Inward repentance. God seeks the broken heart, not the torn robe.
'Turn ye even to me with all your heart'; 'rend your heart, and not your garments,' for He is 'gracious and merciful.' Inward repentance. God seeks the broken heart, not the torn robe.
Who knows if He will turn and leave a blessing? Sanctify a fast and a solemn assembly. Hopeful seeking. Even at the brink, mercy may yet be found.
Who knows if He will turn and leave a blessing? Sanctify a fast and a solemn assembly. Hopeful seeking. Even at the brink, mercy may yet be found.
Gather all the people; let the priests weep and plead, 'Spare thy people, O LORD.' United prayer. The whole nation is called to cry for mercy.
Gather all the people; let the priests weep and plead, 'Spare thy people, O LORD.' United prayer. The whole nation is called to cry for mercy.
Then the LORD is jealous for His land, pities His people, and removes 'the northern army' far off. Mercy answers. God relents toward a humbled people.
Then the LORD is jealous for His land, pities His people, and removes 'the northern army' far off. Mercy answers. God relents toward a humbled people.
Then the LORD is jealous for His land, pities His people, and removes 'the northern army' far off. Mercy answers. God relents toward a humbled people.
Fear not, O land; be glad, for the LORD does great things; pastures spring, trees bear, 'the floors shall be full of wheat.' Plenty restored. Repentance opens the way to renewed blessing.
Fear not, O land; be glad, for the LORD does great things; pastures spring, trees bear, 'the floors shall be full of wheat.' Plenty restored. Repentance opens the way to renewed blessing.
Fear not, O land; be glad, for the LORD does great things; pastures spring, trees bear, 'the floors shall be full of wheat.' Plenty restored. Repentance opens the way to renewed blessing.
Fear not, O land; be glad, for the LORD does great things; pastures spring, trees bear, 'the floors shall be full of wheat.' Plenty restored. Repentance opens the way to renewed blessing.
'I will restore to you the years that the locust hath eaten'; ye shall eat, be satisfied, and praise Him, and never be ashamed. Wasted years redeemed. God can make up what sin and judgment destroyed.
'I will restore to you the years that the locust hath eaten'; ye shall eat, be satisfied, and praise Him, and never be ashamed. Wasted years redeemed. God can make up what sin and judgment destroyed.
'I will restore to you the years that the locust hath eaten'; ye shall eat, be satisfied, and praise Him, and never be ashamed. Wasted years redeemed. God can make up what sin and judgment destroyed.
'I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; your sons and your daughters shall prophesy,' even on servants. Pentecost foretold. Peter declares this fulfilled when the Spirit was poured out.
'I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; your sons and your daughters shall prophesy,' even on servants. Pentecost foretold. Peter declares this fulfilled when the Spirit was poured out.
He will show wonders, the sun darkened, before the great day; 'whosoever shall call on the name of the LORD shall be delivered.' Open door. Salvation is promised to all who call upon Him.
He will show wonders, the sun darkened, before the great day; 'whosoever shall call on the name of the LORD shall be delivered.' Open door. Salvation is promised to all who call upon Him.
